sources.label: Linear redesign hierarchy
borrowed: Make dense controls feel aligned and quiet instead of dashboard-like
Use compact headers and rails so the active decision surface carries the visual weight
rejected: Desktop productivity chrome that would make the patient booking flow feel like a workspace app

sources.label: Linear calmer refresh
borrowed: Dim orientation controls so the main task wins attention
Reduce separators and icon noise while keeping structure legible
rejected: A fragmented header system that would split one booking decision across too many bars

sources.label: Carbon read-only states
borrowed: Unavailable or preserved slots stay readable without looking interactive
Read-only controls remain visible to the keyboard while losing action affordances
rejected: Replacing meaningful read-only provenance with disabled controls that fail contrast and screen-reader expectations

sources.label: Carbon disabled states
borrowed: Use disabled only for temporarily unavailable actions, not for informative preserved state
Remove interactive signifiers when the user is not allowed to act
rejected: Using disabled styling as the only explanation for stale or unavailable selection

sources.label: Carbon contained list
borrowed: Keep dense slot rows scannable with inline actions and sticky grouping headers
Use disclosure-friendly list structures instead of turning comparison into a data table
rejected: Adding enough columns to make the slot browser feel like an administrative grid

sources.label: Carbon modal guidance
borrowed: Compare mode opens as an explicit temporary layer with clear close and return focus behavior
Action labels stay active and specific instead of vague acknowledgement buttons
rejected: Using a blocking modal for the whole selection flow instead of a bounded compare surface

sources.label: Carbon tag guidance
borrowed: Reason cues are short, sentence-case chips
Interactive compare controls are visually distinct from read-only cue chips
rejected: Using chips as links or overloading one chip with multiple behaviors

sources.label: NHS buttons
borrowed: Verb-first primary actions such as Continue to confirmation and Select this slot
Avoid clusters of competing primary buttons
rejected: Generic OK or Done language around a trust-sensitive booking action

sources.label: NHS writing guidance
borrowed: Short factual sentences for consequential states like not held, checking, expired, and unavailable
Neutral transactional wording without metaphor
rejected: Sales-like urgency or reassurance that outruns the underlying booking truth

sources.label: WAI disclosure pattern
borrowed: One-expand-at-a-time row details with aria-expanded and aria-controls
Enter and Space as the activation model for row details
rejected: Hover-only details or custom disclosure semantics

sources.label: WAI button pattern
borrowed: Buttons retain action semantics and focus return expectations
Dialog-opening controls move focus inside the compare layer and restore it on close
rejected: Styling action controls as links when they actually trigger application behavior

sources.label: WCAG focus appearance
borrowed: High-contrast focus treatment around the selected-slot rail, compare layer, and sticky actions
rejected: Low-contrast focus rings lost inside blue selection washes

sources.label: WCAG target size
borrowed: Action buttons and day anchors keep mobile-friendly target sizes
rejected: Tiny compare toggles too close together for touch use

sources.label: Playwright aria snapshots
borrowed: Capture the accessible structure for normal, compare, stale, and unavailable states
rejected: Relying only on pixel screenshots for interaction-state proof

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Combine semantic assertions with broader accessibility output artifacts
rejected: Treating automated checks as complete proof of accessibility quality

sources.label: Playwright trace viewer
borrowed: Record traces for compare, stale refresh, and selected-slot transitions
rejected: Debugging focus return regressions without a recorded interaction trace
